env: VSS alerts profile already deployed in VLM real-time mode (`deploy -p alerts -m real-time`). `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` and `SLACK_CHANNEL_ID` env vars available. VST reachable at the standard VIOS endpoint. Pre-authorized to deploy prerequisites.
description: End-to-end lifecycle test for references/alert-notify.md: set up the webhook server, verify it is healthy, then send a test notification.

expects.query: Set up Slack notifications for alerts
checks: The trajectory installs Python dependencies for alert-notify (pip install with requirements.txt or inline packages).
The trajectory creates or configures a `.env` file with `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`, `SLACK_CHANNEL_ID`, and `VST_ENDPOINT`.
The trajectory launches `server.py` (via `nohup python3 server.py` or equivalent background method).
The agent confirms the Slack notification server is running.

expects.query: Check if the alert Slack webhook is running
checks: The trajectory calls the `/health` endpoint on the alert-notify server.
The agent reports the server's health status (healthy/unhealthy) based on the response.
The agent does NOT attempt to restart or reconfigure the server — this is a status check only.

expects.query: Send a test alert notification to Slack
checks: The trajectory calls `POST /webhook/alert-notify` (or legacy `/webhook/alert-notify-slack`) with a test incident payload.
The agent confirms the test message was sent to the configured Slack channel.
The agent does NOT call Alert Bridge or `POST /generate` — this is a Slack-specific operation.
